title: PipeShare explorer instant cache + fewer parallel file API calls (dbg133)
description:
PipeShare file explorer felt slow on open because every visit refetched the full Wasabi job tree and WinCan/preview work fired parallel GET /api/files/meta and /presign calls (one pair per file id) before the folder list appeared.

This release adds a session-scoped tree cache (memory + sessionStorage, 5 minute TTL) with stale-while-revalidate: cached folders/files render immediately while one background /api/files/tree refresh runs per client+job scope. Explorer no longer sends pathPrefix tree requests on load (folder navigation filters client-side). Duplicate meta/presign pairs are cached for 90 seconds in the browser. Backend adds short private Cache-Control on tree, meta, and presign JSON.

Who is affected: OVH private server on deploy. SaaS when Mike applies the platform version in releases.html.
changeLog: Explorer shows cached job tree instantly; background refresh without blanking the panel.
One tree fetch per client+job (removed pathPrefix-on-load split keys).
Debounced loadRemoteTree (150ms) and defer WinCan DB3 until tree is present.
Browser meta/presign cache cuts duplicate encoded-path API storms on boot.
